description: Low-authority social media/forum posts (Reddit, Facebook) with authority_score < 0.3 and content_length = 0 consistently rank in top-3 positions above high-authority academic and guide sources. This occurs across both factual_lookup and exploratory intents, and across Travel and Education categories.
suggested_fix: Implement a content-availability gate: URLs with has_content=false or content_length < 50 are automatically demoted to position 4+. Additionally, apply domain-type authority penalties for forum_thread and social_media_post page types when higher-authority alternatives exist in the candidate set.

frequency: 33% (1/3 TCs) but 60% of scraped pages in that TC
description: The scrape scoring function assigns L1 floor (0.20) to pages with substantial extracted content (content_length > 20,000) when only cosmetic issues are present, and even to pages with no reported issues at all. This systematically underestimates scrape quality and triggers false contrastive failures on structural_fidelity.
suggested_fix: Re-calibrate scoring thresholds: L1 (0.20) reserved for content_length < 200 or has_content=false; L3 (0.60) minimum for pages with only cosmetic issues; L4 (0.80) minimum for pages with no issues and content_length > 1000.

description: All 3 TCs show scraped markdown beginning with cookie consent banners, navigation links, 'Skip to content' accessibility text, or promotional widgets rather than article content. This degrades both fidelity and structural dimensions and inflates nav_link_ratio.
suggested_fix: Deploy a readability-based main-content extraction step that identifies and isolates the primary content block, stripping navigation, cookie banners, and promotional chrome before markdown generation.

description: Queries containing explicit temporal references ('2026', 'current') retrieve documents that are 2+ years stale or undated, and the ranking model does not penalize this mismatch. Fresh, highly-relevant sources are buried behind stale, lower-relevance sources.
suggested_fix: Extract publication dates from page metadata and content; apply a recency-decay multiplier in ranking for queries with temporal anchors. For year-specific queries, boost documents published within ±1 year of the referenced date by 1.5x.

description: URLs where the scrape pipeline returned has_content=false and content_length=0 were still included in ranking and placed in top-3 positions. The ranking model has no mechanism to detect and penalize empty-content candidates.
suggested_fix: Pass scrape metadata (has_content, content_length) as features to the ranking model. Apply a hard filter: if content_length < 50, exclude from top-3 or apply a 0.3x score multiplier.
